machinery_genes.quote: we identified SpiA (SsaC), an outer membrane protein of SPI-2 TTSS, as a DsbA substrate
machinery_genes.note: SsaC = SpiA. Named here as the outer-membrane component of the SPI-2 T3SS apparatus; Rappl et al 2003 (PMID 14553934) further describe it forming 'the outer membrane secretin' that oligomerises into a functional secretin pore. SctC/secretin role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: Hensel et al 1997 functional analysis of the SPI-2 T3SS apparatus operons. ssaJ maps to Yersinia yscJ = SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaK is named as the first gene of the 10 kb ssaK-U apparatus operon and maps to Yersinia yscL in the abstract; note Hensel's Ysc gene-name alignment is offset by one historical synonym usage. Treated here as a C-ring/base apparatus component (SctK) of the named ssa locus per apparatus brief; low-confidence family assignment, but gene is unambiguously named as an SPI-2 secretion-apparatus gene.

machinery_genes.quote: Here, we investigated the involvement of SPI-2-encoded proteins SsaP (SctP), SsaU (SctU), SsaV (SctV), and SsaL (SctW) in regulation of secretion
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 explicitly equate SsaL with the unified SctW gatekeeper of the SPI-2 T3SS. Also named in Hensel 1997 as part of the ssaK-U operon.

machinery_genes.quote: ssaK, L, M, V, N, O, P, Q, R, S, T, U constitute one operon of 10 kb
machinery_genes.note: ssaM named as a member of the SPI-2 secretion-apparatus operon. Yu et al 2018 (PMID 30279280) describe 'A complex of SsaL, SsaM and SpiC' as the SPI-2 gatekeeper complex. Hensel 1997 notes ssaM has no clear homolog in other T3SS, suggesting an SPI-2-specific accessory role. Included as apparatus, not regulator (it is a secreted/membrane component of the switch machinery), but flagged lower confidence on family.

machinery_genes.quote: Here, we investigated the involvement of SPI-2-encoded proteins SsaP (SctP), SsaU (SctU), SsaV (SctV), and SsaL (SctW) in regulation of secretion
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 equate SsaV with SctV. Hensel 1997 maps ssaV to Yersinia lcrD (= YscV/SctV), the major inner-membrane export-apparatus ring.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaN maps to Yersinia yscN = SctN, the T3SS ATPase.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaO maps to Yersinia yscO = SctO, the ATPase central stalk.

machinery_genes.quote: Here, we investigated the involvement of SPI-2-encoded proteins SsaP (SctP), SsaU (SctU), SsaV (SctV), and SsaL (SctW) in regulation of secretion
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 equate SsaP with SctP (needle-length control / first secretion switch). Also named in the ssaK-U operon by Hensel 1997.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaQ maps to Yersinia yscQ = SctQ, the cytoplasmic C-ring / sorting platform.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaR maps to Yersinia yscR = SctR, an inner-membrane export-gate component.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaS maps to Yersinia yscS = SctS, an inner-membrane export-gate component.

machinery_genes.quote: The deduced products of ssaJ, ssaK, ssaV, ssaN, ssaO, ssaQ, ssaR, ssaS, ssaT, and ssaU show greatest similarity to the Yersinia spp. genes yscJ, yscL, lcrD, yscN, yscO, yscQ, yscR, yscS, yscT, and yscU, respectively
machinery_genes.note: ssaT maps to Yersinia yscT = SctT, an inner-membrane export-gate component.

machinery_genes.quote: Here, we investigated the involvement of SPI-2-encoded proteins SsaP (SctP), SsaU (SctU), SsaV (SctV), and SsaL (SctW) in regulation of secretion
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 equate SsaU with SctU and note 'autocleavage of SsaU' at its conserved NPTH motif drives the first secretion switch. Hensel 1997 maps ssaU to Yersinia yscU.

machinery_genes.quote: SsaG, SsaI, and SsaP are secreted, with SsaG forming the needle structure
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 name SsaG as the protein forming the SPI-2 needle (SctF family). SsaG is the surface needle filament subunit of the SPI-2 injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The ΔssaP mutant hypersecreted SsaI but failed to secrete SseB or SseJ-2HA
machinery_genes.note: Yu et al 2018 refer to SsaI as the SPI-2 inner-rod protein (SctI family); the related Grabe et al 2019 study (PMC6416422) describes 'secretion of the inner rod protein' SsaI. Inner-rod role from apparatus brief (SctI).

machinery_genes.quote: SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular
machinery_genes.note: Nikolaus et al 2001. Translocon proteins are apparatus (they form the host-membrane delivery pore), not effectors, per apparatus brief. SseB forms the surface filament/sheath of the SPI-2 appendage.

machinery_genes.quote: SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular
machinery_genes.note: Nikolaus et al 2001; SseC is a major translocon pore component of the SPI-2 T3SS. Apparatus, not effector.

machinery_genes.quote: SseB, SseC, and SseD function as the translocon for effector proteins by intracellular
machinery_genes.note: Nikolaus et al 2001; SseD is a translocon pore component of the SPI-2 T3SS. Apparatus, not effector.

notes: SPI-2 is the second Salmonella pathogenicity island T3SS (the 'Ssa' injectisome), required for intracellular survival/replication inside the vacuole. The apparatus (ssa genes) is homologous to the Yersinia Ysc/Sct system. The core apparatus operons were defined in S. enterica serovar Typhimurium directly (Hensel et al 1997, Mol Microbiol: ssaJ plus the ssaK-L-M-V-N-O-P-Q-R-S-T-U operon, with explicit Ysc/Sct homology mapping). No strain transfer needed: these are Typhimurium ssa genes, the same locus present in LT2 SPI-2. Coverage: secretin (SsaC/SpiA), inner ring/MS-ring (SsaJ), export gate (SsaR,S,T,U,V), ATPase + stalk (SsaN, SsaO), C-ring/sorting platform (SsaQ; SsaK base), needle (SsaG), inner rod (SsaI), needle-length ruler (SsaP), gatekeeper/switch (SsaL, +SsaM accessory), and the SseB/SseC/SseD translocon. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (the 28 STM effectors in this instance, plus SspH1/SspH2/SseJ etc.); effector chaperones (e.g. SscA/SscB, SseA); the SsrA/SsrB two-component regulator and OmpR/EnvZ; SpiC/SsaB (SsaB=SpiC is a secreted gatekeeper-associated protein with effector-like and regulatory roles, ambiguous classification, so omitted to stay conservative). LOWER-CONFIDENCE CALLS: SsaK family (SctK) and SsaM family assignments rest on operon membership rather than a per-gene structural quote; both are unambiguously named as SPI-2 secretion-apparatus genes but their exact Sct mapping is less certain. Note Hensel 1997's Ysc homology list aligns ssaK->yscL etc., which differs by one synonym from the modern unified Sct table; gene names are quoted verbatim and Sct family labels follow the apparatus brief. All quotes are verbatim from PubMed abstracts (Hensel 1997, Nikolaus 2001, Miki 2004) or the PMC full text of Yu et al 2018 (mBio); all five apparatus DOIs and PMIDs verified via get_article_metadata.
